name: agent-project-kit
purpose: Agent Project Kit: a portable AI project operating kit with Spec–Eval–Loop, WSL2/OneDrive multi-machine workflow, local-resource tracking, machine-profile caching, versioned package updates, token discipline, document production, and conservative Markdown classification that protects serious .md sources and supports archive review/restore. The legacy package/path name is computing-environment.

machines.think: Xeon PC; main heavy-run machine; assumed able to run all projects unless noted
machines.madlab-i9: Core i9 office PC; strong but local resources may differ
machines.black5: Core i7 notebook; portable; prefer light/edit/review/smoke tasks

added: prompts/12_MARKDOWN_CLEANUP.md (renamed conceptually to Markdown Classification and Pipeline Migration)
v6_changes: Markdown is not disposable by default; serious .md files may be canonical source documents.
organize-project-markdown.py now inventories conservatively and archives only high-confidence AI scratch candidates with --apply.
Added --include-archive to inspect previous archive contents and identify restore candidates.
Added --restore-archived to restore over-archived document sources when destination is absent.
Policy renamed conceptually from cleanup to classification/migration.
v6_1_changes: Added .ai/MACHINE_PROFILE.md for first-use machine discovery and minimal resume checks.
Added versioned install/update metadata so package updates do not require rerunning machine discovery.
Installer updates .ai/computing-environment/ and version metadata, but preserves project-local state/profile/resource files.
v6_2_changes: Renamed package identity to Agent Project Kit / agent-project-kit.
Kept computing-environment and .ai/computing-environment as legacy-compatible source and installed snapshot paths.
Added legacy name/path metadata so existing projects can update without migration.
v6_3_changes: Added explicit project hierarchy markers so subdirectories are not treated as projects unless declared.
Added parent-child context flow rules: parent may read child summaries; child reads parent summary only for broad framing.
Added PROJECT_HIERARCHY.md template with examples for nowcast, ting67, and ting67/amt.
v6_4_changes: Added Parent Encapsulation Policy: child projects may read parent summaries/files as a read-only interface.
Child projects must not edit parent files, parent .ai state, parent logs, parent resource manifests, or sibling projects unless explicitly asked.
If child work finds stale parent context, it should propose a parent update instead of silently patching parent state.
v6_5_changes: Added install-from-git.sh and install-from-git.ps1 to clone/update .ai/agent-project-kit and install a local snapshot.
Added GIT_DISTRIBUTION.md and .gitignore for packaging the kit as a GitHub repository.
Installed projects record git repository/ref/commit in .ai/COMPUTING_ENVIRONMENT_VERSION.md.
v6_6_changes: Added AI client adapter files for Claude Code and Antigravity/generic agent IDEs.
Added AI_CLIENTS.md to document adapter strategy and avoid policy drift.
Installers create or append CLAUDE.md and ANTIGRAVITY.md without overwriting existing user files.
v6_7_changes: Added cost profiles C0 Economy, C1 Normal, and C2 Premium alongside token depth modes T0-T3.
Added heavy-work consent rule: ask before high-cost scans, research, rewrites, or test loops when the user appears cost-sensitive.
Updated TOKEN_BUDGET template so new projects can record project-specific token cost defaults.
v6_8_changes: Added OPEN_WITH_AGENT.md for the clone-install-open workflow.
Documented direct clone into .ai/agent-project-kit followed by install-to-project and opening VS Code/Claude Code/Antigravity.
Clarified that agents can read root adapter files after install and continue from project-local .ai state.
v6_9_changes: Reworked README as an install-first public repo entry point.
Separated macOS/Linux, Windows PowerShell, and WSL2 quick-start commands.
Clarified first agent prompt, update flow, token/cost modes, and repository-state policy for public distribution.
